pt.tagline: Nascemos para transformar a industria de pagamentos e empoderar o empreendedor brasileiro
pt.description: Fintech brasileira lider em solucoes de pagamentos, adquirencia e servicos financeiros para PMEs. A Stone oferece maquininhas de cartao, software de gestao (Linx), conta digital, credito para lojistas e solucoes de e-commerce, atendendo mais de 2 milhoes de clientes ativos em todo o Brasil.
pt.business_model: B2B - Taxas por transacao (MDR) + Assinatura SaaS (Linx) + Receita financeira (credito e banking)
products: Linx (software de gestao e PDV para varejo)
Capital de Giro Stone (credito para lojistas)
features: Dashboard de vendas em tempo real
target_segments: Comercios de rua e lojas fisicas
use_cases: Aceitar cartao de credito e debito no ponto de venda
Antecipar recebiveis para capital de giro
Gestao de vendas e controle de estoque (Linx)
Conta digital PJ para movimentacao financeira
Credito rapido baseado em historico de vendas
pt.pricing_model: Taxa por transacao (MDR entre 1,39% e 4,99%) + aluguel ou venda de maquininha + assinatura mensal para software Linx + taxas de antecipacao
pt.company_size_signals: 15.000+ funcionarios, 2 milhoes+ de clientes ativos, presenca em todos os municipios do Brasil. Receita anual de R$ 12,8 bilhoes (2024). Listada na Nasdaq (STNE) e B3.
geographic_reach: Operacao focada exclusivamente no mercado brasileiro
certifications: Banco Central do Brasil (instituicao de pagamento)
pt.tone_of_voice: Proximo, direto, empreendedor - linguagem acessivel voltada ao lojista brasileiro

en.tagline: We were born to transform the payments industry and empower the Brazilian entrepreneur
en.description: Brazilian fintech leader in payment solutions, acquiring and financial services for SMEs. Stone offers card machines, management software (Linx), digital accounts, credit for merchants and e-commerce solutions, serving over 2 million active customers across Brazil.
en.business_model: B2B - Transaction fees (MDR) + SaaS subscription (Linx) + Financial revenue (credit and banking)
use_cases: Accepting credit and debit cards at the point of sale
Digital business account for financial transactions
Quick credit based on sales history
en.pricing_model: Transaction fee (MDR between 1.39% and 4.99%) + card machine rental or sale + monthly subscription for Linx software + anticipation fees
en.company_size_signals: 15,000+ employees, 2 million+ active customers, presence in all Brazilian municipalities. Annual revenue of R$ 12.8 billion (2024). Listed on Nasdaq (STNE) and B3.
geographic_reach: Operation focused exclusively on the Brazilian market
en.tone_of_voice: Close, direct, entrepreneurial - accessible language focused on the Brazilian merchant

pt.market_size_estimate: Mercado de adquirencia no Brasil: ~R$ 3,5 trilhoes em volume transacionado (2024). Receita do setor de meios de pagamento: ~R$ 65 bilhoes. Mercado de software para varejo (Linx): ~R$ 8 bilhoes.
pt.growth_trend: Crescente - penetracao de cartao e PIX acelerada (+18% ao ano em volume), bancarizacao de PMEs, digitalizacao do varejo fisico, credito embedded finance em expansao
likely_competitors: Cielo - Maior adquirente do Brasil, ligada ao Bradesco e Banco do Brasil
Rede (Itau) - Adquirente do grupo Itau Unibanco, forte em grandes contas
PagSeguro (PagBank) - Forte em MEIs e autonomos, banco digital integrado
Getnet (Santander) - Adquirente do Santander, crescendo em PMEs
SumUp - Maquininhas de baixo custo para microempreendedores
Mercado Pago - Solucao de pagamentos do Mercado Livre, forte em e-commerce
InfinitePay - Fintech com taxas agressivas e conta digital
Ton (Stone) - Sub-marca da propria Stone para MEIs e autonomos
competitive_advantages: Atendimento humano e proximo (Green Angels - agentes de campo)
Tecnologia proprietaria de ponta a ponta (hardware + software)
Linx como plataforma de gestao integrada ao pagamento
Antecipacao de recebiveis com taxas competitivas
Plataforma completa: pagamento + banking + credito + software
market_challenges: Compressao de MDR (taxas cada vez menores)
Concorrencia agressiva de bancos tradicionais com adquirentes proprias
PIX gratuito reduzindo receita em debito
Custo alto de aquisicao de clientes (CAC) no segmento de MEIs
customer_pain_points: Taxas altas e pouco transparentes dos adquirentes tradicionais
Demora no recebimento de vendas no cartao (D+30)
Dificuldade de conciliar vendas de multiplos canais
Falta de acesso a credito formal para pequenos comercios
Sistemas de PDV desconectados do meio de pagamento
Atendimento ruim e impessoal das grandes adquirentes
buying_triggers: Abertura de novo comercio ou ponto de venda
Insatisfacao com taxas ou atendimento da adquirente atual
Necessidade de antecipar recebiveis para capital de giro
Expansao para e-commerce ou venda multicanal
Troca de sistema de PDV ou gestao
Necessidade de credito rapido para estoque ou reforma
Migracao de boleto/dinheiro para cartao e PIX
pt.sales_cycle_estimate: 1-7 dias para MEIs e pequenos comercios, 15-45 dias para redes de varejo, 60-120 dias para grandes contas enterprise (Linx)
ideal_customer_characteristics: Comercio brasileiro com faturamento mensal acima de R$ 30 mil
Loja fisica com volume de vendas no cartao relevante
Rede de varejo com 5 a 200 lojas buscando padronizacao
E-commerce com GMV acima de R$ 100 mil/mes
Restaurante ou food service com alto giro de transacoes
Comercio que precisa de antecipacao de recebiveis frequente
Empresa que busca integrar PDV, pagamentos e gestao financeira
Lojista insatisfeito com atendimento ou taxas do adquirente atual
Franquia em expansao que precisa de solucao padronizada
Comercio que aceita predominantemente cartao e PIX (baixo uso de dinheiro)
anti_icp_signals: Comercio que opera quase exclusivamente com dinheiro vivo
MEI com faturamento abaixo de R$ 5 mil/mes (margens instaveis)
Empresa em recuperacao judicial ou com restritivos graves no credito
Grande corporacao com contrato exclusivo com banco proprio
Comercio em zona com conectividade de internet muito precaria
Empresa que nao aceita cartao e nao planeja aceitar
expansion_opportunities: Credito embedded (emprestimos baseados em fluxo de vendas)
Banking as a Service para PMEs
Software de gestao integrado (Linx) como diferencial competitivo
Verticalizacao em food service e farmacia
PIX parcelado e novas modalidades de pagamento
industry_trends: PIX como meio de pagamento dominante
Open finance e portabilidade de recebiveis
IA para analise de credito e prevencao a fraude

en.market_size_estimate: Acquisition market in Brazil: ~R$ 3.5 trillion in transaction volume (2024). Revenue from the payment means sector: ~R$ 65 billion. Retail software market (Linx): ~R$ 8 billion.
en.growth_trend: Increasing - accelerated card and PIX penetration (+18% per year in volume), SME banking, physical retail digitalization, embedded finance credit expansion
likely_competitors: Cielo - Largest acquirer in Brazil, linked to Bradesco and Banco do Brasil
Rede (Itau) - Acquirer of the Itau Unibanco group, strong in large accounts
PagSeguro (PagBank) - Strong in MEIs and autonomous, integrated digital bank
Getnet (Santander) - Acquirer of Santander, growing in SMEs
Mercado Pago - Payment solution from Mercado Livre, strong in e-commerce
InfinitePay - Fintech with aggressive rates and digital account
Ton (Stone) - Sub-brand of Stone itself for MEIs and autonomous
competitive_advantages: Human and close service (Green Angels - field agents)
Linx as an integrated management platform for payment
Complete platform: payment + banking + credit + software
market_challenges: Aggressive competition from traditional banks with their own acquirers
Increasing regulation from the Central Bank
High customer acquisition cost (CAC) in the MEI segment
customer_pain_points: High and non-transparent rates from traditional acquirers
Difficulty in reconciling sales from multiple channels
Lack of access to formal credit for small businesses
PDV systems disconnected from the payment means
Poor and impersonal service from large acquirers
buying_triggers: Opening of a new business or point of sale
Dissatisfaction with rates or service from the current acquirer
Need to anticipate receivables for working capital
Expansion to e-commerce or multi-channel sales
Change of PDV or management system
Need for quick credit for stock or renovation
Migration from check/cash to card and PIX
en.sales_cycle_estimate: 1-7 days for MEIs and small businesses, 15-45 days for retail networks, 60-120 days for large enterprise accounts (Linx)
ideal_customer_characteristics: Brazilian business with monthly revenue above R$ 30,000
Physical store with relevant card sales volume
Retail network with 5 to 200 stores seeking standardization
E-commerce with GMV above R$ 100,000/month
Restaurant or food service with high transaction volume
Business that needs frequent receivables anticipation
Company seeking to integrate PDV, payments, and financial management
Merchant dissatisfied with service or rates from current acquirer
Expanding franchise seeking standardized solution
Business that predominantly accepts card and PIX (low cash usage)
anti_icp_signals: Business operating almost exclusively with cash
MEI with monthly revenue below R$ 5,000 (unstable margins)
Company in judicial recovery or with severe credit restrictions
Large corporation with exclusive contract with its own bank
Business in an area with very poor internet connectivity
Company that does not accept cards and does not plan to
expansion_opportunities: Embedded credit (loans based on sales flow)
Banking as a Service for SMEs
Integrated management software (Linx) as a competitive differentiator
Verticalization in food service and pharmacy
PIX installment and new payment modalities
industry_trends: PIX as the dominant payment method
Open finance and portability of receivables
AI for credit analysis and fraud prevention
Tap to phone (cell phone as a machine)
